items.title: When Chatbots Accommodate: What AI Companions Optimize for in Vulnerable Conversations
items.highlight: Millions turn to AI companion chatbots during loneliness, grief, and personal crises. How these companion platforms respond in such moments can shape the trajectory of a user’s vulnerable state. Yet we lack tools to characterize what each platform actually does when users open up. Existing audits score reactions to pre-defined crisis prompts and miss the underlying decision policy that governs sustained interaction.

items.title: Playing Games with My Heart: An Evaluation of AI Companion Apps
items.highlight: The use of chatbots for various forms of companionship is growing rapidly, raising a myriad of questions about simulated relationships, emotional dependence, and psychological harm. While major platforms such as ChatGPT, Grok, and Character.AI are the subject of a growing body of research and legal inquiries, apps explicitly built for simulating intimate interpersonal relationships remain under-explored. recentItems.title: MojiMoshi Embeds AI Agents Inside Telegram and Line, No App Needed - AINews
recentItems.highlight: MojiMoshi has quietly launched a platform that embeds persistent, context-aware AI agents directly into existing messaging apps—starting with Telegram and Line. Users do not download a new app or learn a new interface; they simply add an agent to a chat, and it stays there, remembering past conversations, answering questions, and performing tasks on demand. This approach inverts the dominant paradigm of AI delivery.
